pokemon:lickilicky.name: Lickilicky
pokemon:lickilicky.summary: Their saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Their saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.
The long tongue is always soggy with slobber. The saliva contains a solvent that causes numbness.
Their saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.
It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.
Its saliva can decompose anything. It wraps its long tongue around things to coat them with its sticky saliva.
Its tongue can extend and contract freely, and it is capable of reaching lengths over 10 times Lickilicky's height. Beware of the saliva, as it contains corrosive elements.
It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.
It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.
The long tongue is always soggy with slobber. The saliva contains a solvent that causes numbness.
Lickilicky can do just about anything with its tongue, which is as dexterous as the human hand. In contrast, Lickilicky’s use of its fingers is clumsy.
It has space in its throat to store saliva. It can also roll up its tongue and store it in the same spot.
Lickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.
It uses its tongue much more skillfully than its hands or its feet. It can deftly pick up a single small bean with its tongue.
A contest is under way to determine which one can stick its tongue out the farthest. The current record is...more than 82 feet.
The long tongue is always soggy with slobber. The saliva contains a solvent that causes numbness.
Their saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.
It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.
Their saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Their saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.

pokemon:lickitung.name: Lickitung
pokemon:lickitung.summary: Whenever Lickitung comes across something new, it will unfailingly give it a lick. It does so because it memorizes things by texture and by taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Whenever Lickitung comes across something new, it will unfailingly give it a lick. It does so because it memorizes things by texture and by taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
When it extends its over-six-foot- long tongue, its tail quivers. There is a possibility they are connected.
Being licked by its long, saliva-covered tongue leaves a tingling sensation. Extending its tongue retracts its tail.
Its tongue can be extended like a chameleon's. It leaves a tingling sensation when it licks enemies.
It has a tongue that is over 6’6’’ long. It uses this long tongue to lick its body clean.
Instead of hands, it uses its tongue, which is twice its height. Its sticky saliva grips anything.
Whenever it sees something unfamiliar, it always licks the object because it memorizes things by texture and taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
Its tongue is twice the length of its body. It can be moved like an arm for grabbing food and attacking.
Its tongue has well-developed nerves that run to the very tip, so it can be deft­ ly manipulated.
Its tongue has well-developed nerves that run to the very tip, so it can be deftly manipulated.
Its tongue can be extended like a chameleon’s. It leaves a tingling sensation when it licks enemies.
Wields its long tongue deftly, as though it were an arm. The Pokémon's viscous saliva, once it has been collected and boiled down, yields a strong and highly useful adhesive.
Its tongue spans almost seven feet and moves more freely than its forelegs. Its licks can cause paralysis.
Its tongue spans almost seven feet and moves more freely than its forelegs. Its licks can cause paralysis.
Whenever Lickitung comes across something new, it will unfailingly give it a lick. It does so because it memorizes things by texture and by taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
Instead of hands, it uses its tongue, which is twice its height. Its sticky saliva grips anything.
When it extends its over-six-foot- long tongue, its tail quivers. There is a possibility they are connected.
Its tongue can be extended like a chameleon's. It leaves a tingling sensation when it licks enemies.
Whenever LICKITUNG comes across something new, it will unfailingly give it a lick. It does so because it memorizes things by texture and by taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
Whenever LICKITUNG comes across something new, it will unfailingly give it a lick. It does so because it memorizes things by texture and by taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
Bug Pokémon are Lickitung’s main food source. This Pokémon paralyzes its prey with a lick from its long tongue, then swallows the prey whole.
Its long tongue, slathered with a gooey saliva, sticks to any­ thing, so it is very useful.
Its long tongue, slathered with a gooey saliva, sticks to anything, so it is very useful.
If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.
It licks filth clean with its tongue. Whatever it licks always stinks afterward, so whether it’s really clean is...questionable.
It checks out whatever’s around it by licking everything. If you don’t clean off a spot where it’s licked you, you’ll break out in a rash!
When it extends its over-six-foot- long tongue, its tail quivers. There is a possibility they are connected.
Being licked by its long, saliva-covered tongue leaves a tingling sensation. Extending its tongue retracts its tail.
Its long tongue, slathered with a gooey saliva, sticks to anything, so it is very useful.
Its tongue is twice the length of its body. It can be moved like an arm for grabbing food and attacking.
Its tongue spans almost 7 feet and moves more freely than its forelegs. Its licks can cause paralysis.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Whenever Lickitung comes across something new, it will unfailingly give it a lick. It does so because it memorizes things by texture and by taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
